channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: YAML Formatter: a YAML Formatter tool that never sees your data
item.description: Most online YAML Formatter tools quietly send what you paste to a server. That's fine until it isn't — config blobs, tokens, and API responses are exactly the kind of thing you don't want leaving...

item.title: Rate Limiting Done Right: Respecting X's Anti-Abuse Stack
item.description: Most rate limiting articles teach you how to enforce rate limits on your API. This article is about the other side: how to respect rate limits when your application is a client of someone else's...

item.title: Variables and Data Types in Python
item.description: Variables and Data Types in Python: Store and Use Data Like a Pro In Python, variables are simply names that refer to values. Understanding the main data types makes it easier to choose the right...

item.title: Day 25 - Helm Chart
item.description: Kubernetes is powerful, but managing Kubernetes YAML files manually becomes difficult very quickly. A simple application may need: Deployment Service ConfigMap Secret Ingress HPA ServiceAccount...

item.title: Preventing Cloud Configuration Drift Without the Headache of State Files
item.description: The transition to Infrastructure as Code revolutionized how engineering teams deploy and manage cloud resources. Instead of relying on error-prone manual processes and endless clicks within a web...

item.title: BoxAgnts Runtime (5) — MCP Is Just the Beginning, the Runtime Layer Is What Matters
item.description: The emergence of MCP (Model Context Protocol) marks a major milestone for the AI ecosystem. For the first time, the industry is converging around a shared interface for tool...

item.title: How I built a Nepali Unicode-Preeti converter
item.description: How I Built a Nepali Unicode-Preeti Converter Nepal has a unique font problem. Modern Nepali text uses Unicode — the international standard, works everywhere, renders natively on all devices. But...

item.title: Your resume says "K8s." The job description says "Kubernetes." The ATS marks it as missing.
item.description: A senior engineer with five years of production experience ships a resume. React, TypeScript, AWS, Kubernetes: all of it real, all of it daily. Strong GitHub. Clean system design instincts....

item.title: JSON ↔ CSV: a JSON ↔ CSV tool that never sees your data
item.description: Most online JSON ↔ CSV tools quietly send what you paste to a server. That's fine until it isn't — config blobs, tokens, and API responses are exactly the kind of thing you don't want leaving your...

item.title: BAGEN: LLM Agents Waste 44% of Tokens on Tasks They'll Fail
item.description: You're paying for every token your agent burns. And according to new research from Northwestern, Stanford, Cornell, and All Hands AI, a large share of that spend goes directly to waste — on...

item.title: Valve says it’s ready to launch the Steam Machine this summer
item.description: Valve now says that the delayed Steam Machine PC and Steam Frame VR headset are set to launch sometime this summer. In a Thursday blog post detailing its Verified programs for both pieces of...

item.title: Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns
item.description: Anthropic has been growing at a breakneck pace. The company announced that annualized revenue crossed $47 billion in May, up dramatically from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025. That...

item.title: Airbnb’s Brian Chesky plans to launch a new AI lab
item.description: The Airbnb CEO said last year it hasn't struck an LLM partnership because existing products weren't quite ready.

item.title: Defense tech, AI, and fundraising take center stage at StrictlyVC Los Angeles on June 18
item.description: On Thursday, June 18, at The Aerospace Corporation Campus, investors, founders, and tech leaders will gather for an evening of conversation exploring some of the most consequential shifts taking...

item.title: Cyberdecks used to look like little laptops, but now they’re getting more personal
item.description: DIYer and TikTok user Annike Tan, who goes by @ubeboobey, can carry her cyberdeck around without anyone noticing because it doesn't look like a computer at all. Tan, who has been featured in The...

item.title: Dashlane explains how attackers managed to download encrypted password vaults
item.description: By targeting large numbers of users, attackers increased their chances of success.

item.title: Meta steals a tactic from Tesla and builds data centers in tents
item.description: Meta may have found one way to slash its massive data center bill: tents.

item.title: Apple approves Poke as the first AI agent on its Messages for Business platform
item.description: Poke, the startup that lets people use AI agents through simple text messages, has become the first AI agent approved for Apple’s Messages for Business platform.

item.title: Nemotron 3.5 Content Safety: Customizable Multimodal Safety for Global Enterprise AI
The AI IPO Race Heats Up, DOGE Whistleblower Sues Elon Musk, and Instagram Gets Hacked
item.description: On Uncanny Valley, we dive into the IPO bonanza that the top AI companies are embarking on to the point where some real estate listings are looking for not just regular old cash, but Anthropic stock.

item.title: Kevin O’Leary agrees to downsize massive Utah data center
item.description: Kevin O'Leary agreed to halve the size of his planned 40,000-acre data center in Utah amid mounting pressure from residents and activists, as reported earlier by local affiliate ABC4. The Shark...

item.title: Google is letting social media stars customize their search result page
item.description: Google now lets big creators and publishers in the US claim dedicated profiles in Search to highlight things like videos, articles, and their other profiles online. But this feature won't be...

item.title: Amazon’s new plan for games: James Bond and AI Snoop Dogg
item.description: Amazon's gaming strategy has never really been clear. It's been very active in the space: acquiring Twitch, launching its Luna cloud gaming service nearly six years ago, investing heavily in MMOs...

item.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
item.description: Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root. It is tracked as...

item.title: Meta rolls out a new AI creator assistant on Facebook
item.description: Creators often have to parse through charts and dashboards to understand their performance, but with the new AI assistant, they can get quick answers to questions like "When should I post?" and...

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

item.title: Belkin’s new Joy-Con grips also boost the Switch 2’s battery life
item.description: Most of Belkin's Switch 2 accessories are designed to either protect or power up Nintendo's latest handheld, like its Charging Case Pro that actually does both at the same time. Its new...

item.title: Cash App made a magic wand for contactless payments
item.description: The convenience of contactless payments can already feel magical, but Cash App is really leaning into that with its latest accessory. The mobile payment service is launching the Cash App Wand: an...

item.title: Windows is back on the Microsoft menu
item.description: I can't remember the last time Microsoft kicked off a Build keynote with Windows front and center, but that's exactly what CEO Satya Nadella did this week. Nadella didn't address the issues...

item.title: Remarkable’s refurbished Paper Pro bundle is hundreds off
item.description: Color E Ink tablets aren’t usually affordable. I’m not going to say that Woot’s price on a refurbished “good as new” Remarkable Paper Pro is cheap, but it’s pretty fantastic compared to buying one...
